description: Replace the token-splice transpiler with a real lexer/parser/AST front-end, retire the splice passes behind a new Go backend, and move every tool (check, fix, fmt, LSP) onto the shared AST.

userStories.title: Add corpus Case model and loader
userStories.description: As a compiler engineer, I need a runner-independent model of golden cases so the suite is decoupled from package layout.
acceptanceCriteria: internal/corpus defines Case{ID, Kind(transpile|check|doctest), Input, Expected, Mode(file|package), Normalize} and Load(path)(Manifest,error)
A unit test loads a small fixture manifest from internal/corpus/testdata and asserts every field of one case of each Kind

userStories.title: Generate manifest over existing corpus
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need the existing golden files indexed into one manifest so any runner can consume them.
acceptanceCriteria: A generator walks features/NN/examples, testdata, and testdata/check and writes corpus/manifest.json without moving any source file
A test asserts the generated manifest contains 51 transpile pairs and 50 check cases (the current corpus counts)
userStories.notes: Counts from audit: 40 feature examples + 11 testdata = 51 transpile pairs; 50 check cases.

userStories.title: Build interface-based transpile runner
userStories.description: As a compiler engineer, I need a transpile runner behind an interface so multiple front-ends can be judged identically.
acceptanceCriteria: internal/corpus defines Transpiler interface{ Transpile(src string)(pipeline.Output,error) } and a runner that gofmt-normalizes both got and want before comparing
A test runs every transpile case in the manifest against pipeline.Transpile and all pass

userStories.title: Build interface-based check runner
userStories.description: As a compiler engineer, I need a check runner that honors inline // want markers so checker cases are runner-independent.
acceptanceCriteria: Runner defines Checker interface and matches each // want "substr" marker against a diagnostic on the same line, failing on any unclaimed Error-severity diagnostic
A test runs every check case in the manifest against check.Analyze and all pass

userStories.title: Build doctest sidecar runner
userStories.description: As a compiler engineer, I need doctest cases compared via the manifest so the doctest output is part of the same suite.
acceptanceCriteria: Runner compares Output.Test to the .go.expected sidecar (gofmt-normalized) for every doctest case in the manifest
A test runs all doctest cases against pipeline.Transpile and all pass

userStories.title: Rewire existing harnesses to runner
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need the old test harnesses to delegate to the corpus runner so hardcoded paths disappear.
acceptanceCriteria: pipeline_test.go and check_test.go contain no hardcoded feature-dir lists or filepath.Join("..","..","features",...) paths (grep returns nothing)
The full suite remains green after the rewrite

userStories.title: Add behavioral tier: compile output
userStories.description: As a compiler engineer, I need generated Go compiled per case so conformance is judged by behavior, not Go spelling.
acceptanceCriteria: For every transpile case the runner writes Output.Go to a temp module and runs go build + go vet on it
A test asserts every transpile case's generated Go builds and vets cleanly
userStories.notes: Reuse the temp-dir machinery from goal build. This tier is implementation-independent and gates all later phases.

userStories.title: Add behavioral tier: run doctests
userStories.description: As a compiler engineer, I need doctest sidecars executed so behavior is proven, not just compiled.
acceptanceCriteria: For each case that has a doctest sidecar, the runner writes both files to a temp module and runs go test on it
A test asserts every doctest-bearing case passes go test in its temp module

userStories.title: Reify package-mode tests as fixtures
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need the inline package-mode tests as on-disk fixtures so they live in the manifest.
acceptanceCriteria: foreign_test.go and pipeline_package_test.go inline sources become on-disk multi-file fixtures with a declared import map, indexed as Mode=package cases
The runner executes the package-mode cases and they pass
userStories.notes: Fixtures under testdata/package/<name>/ with a pkg.json (name + import map). Generate indexes them as Kind=transpile, Mode=package via Case.Package (PackageSpec). corpus.RunPackage transpiles + validates Go + builds each package (foreign import wired into a temp module). Manifest now 107 cases (51+50+4+2).

userStories.title: Archive legacy per-feature transpilers
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need the superseded standalone transpilers archived so they stop implying they are live.
acceptanceCriteria: features/NN/transpiler directories are moved under attic/ and no features/*/transpiler path remains
go build ./... and go test ./... remain green (these were separate modules excluded from the root module)

userStories.title: Add token package with positions
userStories.description: As a compiler engineer, I need a token type with line/col positions so the AST can carry real source positions.
acceptanceCriteria: internal/token defines Kind constants for every goal lexeme and Pos{Offset,Line,Col}
A unit test asserts Kind/String round-trips and Pos ordering

userStories.title: Lex core Go tokens with positions
userStories.description: As a compiler engineer, I need a lexer for the Go subset so ordinary source tokenizes with correct positions.
acceptanceCriteria: internal/lexer tokenizes identifiers, literals, operators, and delimiters with accurate Offset/Line/Col
A test tokenizes a multi-line sample and asserts the kind and position of each token

userStories.title: Lex goal-specific lexemes and trivia
userStories.description: As a compiler engineer, I need the lexer to emit goal lexemes so the parser need not fake them.
acceptanceCriteria: Lexer emits single tokens for ? , => , and ... ; retains /// doc-comment content as trivia; and lexes implements/sealed/from/derive as identifiers
A test asserts each of these lexemes produces exactly one token of the expected kind and that comments attach as trivia
userStories.notes: => must be one token, not '=' then '>' as the splice passes treat it.

userStories.title: Define core AST nodes and Walk
userStories.description: As a compiler engineer, I need AST node types for the Go subset plus a visitor so tools can traverse one tree.
acceptanceCriteria: internal/ast defines File, the Go decl/stmt/expr/type nodes goal uses, and Walk(Visitor,Node)
A test builds a tree by hand and asserts Walk visits every node exactly once

userStories.title: Add goal declaration AST nodes
userStories.description: As a compiler engineer, I need AST nodes for goal declarations so enums and friends are first-class.
acceptanceCriteria: ast defines EnumDecl/Variant/PayloadField, SealedInterfaceDecl, ImplementsClause, and from/derive FuncDecl modifiers
A test asserts Walk descends into each new node's children

userStories.title: Add goal expression and pattern nodes
userStories.description: As a compiler engineer, I need AST nodes for match, ? and construction so the three meanings of Enum.Variant(x) are distinct node types.
acceptanceCriteria: ast defines MatchExpr/MatchArm, VariantPattern/RestPattern, UnwrapExpr, VariantLit/LabeledArg, and SpreadElement
A test asserts a construction VariantLit and a destructuring VariantPattern are distinct node types and both walk correctly
userStories.notes: This is the structural fix for the Match-before-Enums ordering hack.

userStories.title: Parse package, imports, declarations
userStories.description: As a compiler engineer, I need top-level declaration parsing so a file's structure becomes an AST.
acceptanceCriteria: internal/parser parses package clause, imports, and func/type/var/const declarations of the Go subset into ast.File
A test parses a sample file and asserts the declaration list shape

userStories.title: Parse statements
userStories.description: As a compiler engineer, I need statement parsing so function bodies become AST.
acceptanceCriteria: Parser handles assignment, short-var, if/for/switch/return/defer/go and block statements
A test parses a function body with each statement form and asserts the statement list

userStories.title: Parse expressions with Pratt and postfix ?
userStories.description: As a compiler engineer, I need precedence-correct expression parsing so postfix ? falls out of the grammar.
acceptanceCriteria: Parser parses binary/unary/selector/call/index expressions with correct precedence and postfix ? as UnwrapExpr
A test asserts f(x)? , a.b? , and a mixed-precedence binary expression parse to the expected tree shape

userStories.title: Parse enum, sealed, implements
userStories.description: As a compiler engineer, I need enum-family parsing so closed types become AST.
acceptanceCriteria: Parser parses enum declarations (data-less and payload variants), sealed interface, and struct implements clauses
A test parses the 01-enums and 07-implements example inputs and asserts variant/field/implements structure

userStories.title: Parse match and patterns
userStories.description: As a compiler engineer, I need match parsing in both positions so value-position match is representable.
acceptanceCriteria: Parser parses match as both a statement and an expression (var x = match ...), including binding and rest patterns
A test asserts a statement-position and a value-position match both parse with the expected arms
userStories.notes: Value-position match is refused by the current splice approach; it must parse here.

userStories.title: Parse construction, labeled args, spread
userStories.description: As a compiler engineer, I need construction parsing so Enum.Variant(field: v) and ...defaults become AST.
acceptanceCriteria: Parser parses VariantLit with LabeledArg arguments and SpreadElement (...defaults, ...derive(s)) inside composite literals
A test parses Status.Active(since: now()) and a literal containing ...defaults and asserts the nodes

userStories.title: Parse from/derive, assert, doctests
userStories.description: As a compiler engineer, I need the remaining goal constructs parsed so the grammar is complete.
acceptanceCriteria: Parser parses from func and derive func (bodyless and bodied), assert statements, and /// doctest comments as structured nodes
A test parses the 10-assert, 11-doctests, and 12-derive-convert example inputs and asserts their nodes
userStories.notes: Parser now parses from/derive func modifiers (reusing FuncDecl.Mod/ModPos + optional body), assert (bare + printf-message, top-level-comma split), and /// doctests as structured nodes (DocComment/Doctest attached to FuncDecl.Doc). New ast.AssertStmt. DOC_COMMENT tokens are retained in the stream (not stripped) and collected before each decl.

userStories.title: Gate: parse 100% of corpus
userStories.description: As a compiler engineer, I need every corpus input to parse so the parser is proven complete against the spec.
acceptanceCriteria: A test iterates every .goal input in the corpus manifest and parses it with zero errors
The test fails loudly listing any input that does not parse
userStories.notes: Parser now parses 100% of the corpus (104 unique .goal inputs across 107 manifest cases). Closed 4 grammar gaps: multi-element type-arg/index lists (new ast.IndexListExpr; single index keeps IndexExpr); type-literal operands in expression position ([]byte(p) conversions, map[K]V{}/[]T{} composites) via parseOperand+compositeOK; optional-colon enum payload fields (Active { since int }); brace-less statement match-arm bodies (=> return true) via parseMatchArm dispatching parseStmt on statement keywords. Gate test: corpus.TestParseGate enumerates every unique input and lists any that fail.

userStories.title: Add AST snapshot tests
userStories.description: As a compiler engineer, I need stable AST snapshots so parser structure is pinned per construct.
acceptanceCriteria: Parser can render an AST to a deterministic textual form (e.g. s-expression) and snapshot goldens exist for one input per goal construct
A test compares the rendered AST of each representative input to its checked-in snapshot
userStories.notes: Replaces the tree-sitter differential as the loop-ready gate; grammar.js remains a manual cross-check reference. ast.Sexpr (internal/ast/dump.go) renders a position-free, deterministic s-expression via reflection (skips token.Pos, names token.Kind/FuncMod/ChanDir, elides zero fields); internal/parser TestASTSnapshots parses one representative input per goal construct and diffs against testdata/snapshots/*.sexpr (-update-snapshots regenerates).

userStories.title: Add Backend and Formatter interfaces
userStories.description: As a compiler engineer, I need pluggable backend/formatter seams so the new engine can run behind a flag.
acceptanceCriteria: Backend interface{ Emit(*ast.File,*sema.Info)(Output,error) } and Formatter interface exist; the driver selects engine via a flag (e.g. --engine=ast)
A test transpiles a goal file that uses no goal-specific constructs through the new engine and the output compiles via the behavioral tier
userStories.notes: Phase 2 seam. internal/sema (placeholder Info+New, filled by US-027); internal/backend defines Backend{Emit(*ast.File,*sema.Info)(pipeline.Output,error)} + Formatter{Format([]byte)([]byte,error)} + GoFormatter (go/format) + goBackend (minimal plain-Go emitter, emit.go) + Transpile engine (parse->sema->Emit->format, satisfies corpus.Transpiler). Driver --engine=splice|ast flag in parseFlags; ast routes per-file via backend.Transpile (transpilePackageAST); splice unchanged + default. AC2 proven by corpus.RunCompile over testdata/plain.goal. Emitter errors on goal-specific/unsupported nodes (US-032+).

userStories.title: Resolve symbols by AST walk
userStories.description: As a compiler engineer, I need name-keyed facts derived from the AST so analyze's token scanning is retired.
acceptanceCriteria: internal/sema builds enums/structs/signatures/from-registry/methods by walking the AST
A test asserts sema resolves the same symbols as analyze.Build for a set of representative inputs, including a struct field whose type contains an embedded comma (the analyze comma-split bug)
userStories.notes: Fixes the known analyze.parseStructBody comma-split bug structurally.

userStories.title: Wrap go/types depth checks behind interface
userStories.description: As a compiler engineer, I need the go/types crutch behind a TypeChecker interface so it can be swapped later without caller changes.
acceptanceCriteria: TypeChecker interface is defined and the existing typecheck (transpile then go/types) implements it
A test exercises the depth checks through the interface and the existing typecheck cases still pass
userStories.notes: Decision 4: no native goal type checker until the runtime forces it. internal/typecheck now defines TypeChecker{Check(pkg *project.Package)([]Diagnostic,error)} keyed on diagnostics (NOT Load's *types.Package, so a native checker with no go/types view can satisfy it); GoTypesChecker is the transpile-then-go/types crutch (Load + CheckImplements/CheckMustUse/CheckNoZeroValue). cmd/goal runDepthChecks routes through the interface value, so the implementation can flip with no caller change. Load + Check* stay exported and unchanged.

userStories.title: Reimplement exhaustiveness check on AST
userStories.description: As a goal developer, I need exhaustiveness checking over the AST so match diagnostics survive the front-end change.
acceptanceCriteria: sema implements the match exhaustiveness check over the AST
Every exhaustiveness-related case in testdata/check passes through the sema checker via the corpus runner

userStories.title: Reimplement field-completeness check on AST
userStories.description: As a goal developer, I need no-zero-value (section 8) checking over the AST so field diagnostics survive.
acceptanceCriteria: sema implements the field-completeness check over the AST (deferrals as Warnings, violations as Errors)
Every field-completeness case in testdata/check passes through the sema checker via the corpus runner

userStories.title: Reimplement mustuse/implements/question checks
userStories.description: As a goal developer, I need the remaining lexical checks over the AST so all diagnostics survive.
acceptanceCriteria: sema implements must-use, implements, and ?-arity/refusal checks over the AST
Every must-use, implements, and question case in testdata/check passes through the sema checker via the corpus runner

userStories.title: Emit the Go subset from AST
userStories.description: As a compiler engineer, I need a code emitter for ordinary Go so the backend can print non-goal code.
acceptanceCriteria: backend/go walks the AST and emits Go source text for the Go subset, formatted once via Formatter
A goal file using only plain-Go constructs transpiles through the new backend and passes the behavioral tier

userStories.title: Lower and emit enums and implements
userStories.description: As a goal developer, I need enum/sealed/implements lowering on the new backend so closed types work.
acceptanceCriteria: lower+backend produce the sum encoding for enums and the assertion/marker for implements
The 01-enums and 07-implements transpile cases pass the behavioral tier through the new backend

userStories.title: Lower and emit Result and Option
userStories.description: As a goal developer, I need open-E Result and Option lowering on the new backend so the core types work.
acceptanceCriteria: lower+backend produce (T,error) for open-E Result and *T for Option, including statement-position match over them
The 03-result and 04-option transpile cases pass the behavioral tier through the new backend

userStories.title: Lower and emit ? with real gensym
userStories.description: As a compiler engineer, I need ? propagation lowered with proper gensym so the magic __goal_ prefix is retired.
acceptanceCriteria: ? propagation is lowered using scope-aware generated identifiers, not the literal __goal_ prefix
The 05-question-prop cases pass the behavioral tier and a test asserts the generated Go contains no __goal_ substring

userStories.title: Lower and emit match including value position
userStories.description: As a goal developer, I need full match lowering so value-position match finally works.
acceptanceCriteria: lower+backend emit a type-switch for enum match in both statement and value position
The 02-match cases plus a new value-position-match corpus case pass the behavioral tier through the new backend
userStories.notes: Add the new value-position-match example to the corpus as part of this story.

userStories.title: Lower and emit closed-E Result
userStories.description: As a goal developer, I need closed-E Result lowering so sum-encoded errors work on the new backend.
acceptanceCriteria: lower+backend emit the Ok/Err sum constructors, closed match, closed ?, and the From-conversion, with the prelude emitted once
The 06-error-e cases pass the behavioral tier through the new backend

userStories.title: Lower and emit defaults and assert
userStories.description: As a goal developer, I need ...defaults and assert lowering so those constructs work on the new backend.
acceptanceCriteria: lower+backend expand ...defaults to explicit zero values and assert to an if-panic guard
The 08-no-zero-value and 10-assert cases pass the behavioral tier through the new backend

userStories.title: Lower and emit derive/from
userStories.description: As a goal developer, I need derive/from lowering so type-directed conversion works on the new backend.
acceptanceCriteria: lower+backend expand derive func field-by-field using resolved types (not string parsing) and strip the from modifier
The 12-derive-convert cases pass the behavioral tier through the new backend

userStories.title: Emit doctest sidecars on new path
userStories.description: As a goal developer, I need doctests extracted and emitted by the new backend so executable docs survive.
acceptanceCriteria: The new backend extracts /// doctests and emits the _test.go sidecar lowered through the same path
The 11-doctests cases pass the doctest tier through the new backend

userStories.title: Gate: full behavioral parity
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need the entire corpus green through the new backend so the flip is safe.
acceptanceCriteria: Every transpile, check, and doctest case passes the behavioral conformance tier through the new (AST) engine
A single test runs the whole manifest under the new engine and reports zero failures

userStories.title: Regenerate goldens and make AST default
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need exact goldens regenerated and the new engine defaulted so the new path is canonical.
acceptanceCriteria: The .go.expected goldens are regenerated from the new backend and the manifest exact tier is green against the new default engine
The old splice engine remains available behind a flag for one release
userStories.notes: New gensym names and equivalent encodings mean exact goldens necessarily change here.

userStories.title: Delete splice passes and dead scanning
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need the obsolete splice machinery removed so there is one front-end.
acceptanceCriteria: internal/pass, scan's Splice machinery, and the analyze token-scan tables subsumed by sema are deleted; grep finds no references to them
Build, vet, and the full suite are green after removal

userStories.title: Move goal fix onto the AST
userStories.description: As a goal developer, I need fix to operate on the AST so it shares one model with the compiler.
acceptanceCriteria: internal/fix consumes the AST instead of re-lexing, and emits the same rewrites
The existing fix test suite passes unchanged in observable behavior

userStories.title: Add goal fmt source printer
userStories.description: As a goal developer, I need goal fmt so goal source has a canonical, comment-preserving format.
acceptanceCriteria: goal fmt prints goal source from the AST preserving comments, and is idempotent
A test asserts fmt(fmt(src)) == fmt(src) for every corpus .goal input and that comments are retained on a sample
userStories.notes: This is the one place comment/whitespace fidelity matters; use position-carrying nodes + comment attachment.

userStories.title: Switch LSP document model to AST
userStories.description: As an editor user, I need the LSP backed by the AST so richer features become possible.
acceptanceCriteria: internal/lsp diagnostics and document symbols are derived from the AST (lsp/symbols.go scanDecls token walk removed)
The existing LSP test suite passes

userStories.title: Add LSP semantic tokens
userStories.description: As an editor user, I need semantic tokens so goal constructs are highlighted by role.
acceptanceCriteria: The server advertises and serves textDocument/semanticTokens classified from the AST
A test asserts the token classification of a sample containing an enum, a match, and a ? expression

userStories.title: Add LSP go-to-definition
userStories.description: As an editor user, I need go-to-definition so I can navigate goal symbols.
acceptanceCriteria: textDocument/definition resolves a referenced symbol to its declaration position via the AST symbol graph
A test asserts definition of a function call and an enum variant resolve to the expected positions

userStories.title: Add LSP hover with types
userStories.description: As an editor user, I need hover so I can see a symbol's type and doc.
acceptanceCriteria: textDocument/hover returns the type and any doc comment for a symbol under the cursor
A test asserts hover over a Result-returning function reports its signature

userStories.title: Add LSP rename and find references
userStories.description: As an editor user, I need rename and find-references so I can refactor goal safely.
acceptanceCriteria: textDocument/references and textDocument/rename return all reference sites / workspace edits via the AST symbol graph
A test asserts renaming a symbol produces edits at every reference in a sample
